# observable

```go
import "github.com/radiospiel/simple-go/src/observable"
```

Package observable provides a data wrapper for maps and lists with path\-based access and change subscriptions.

## Variables

<a name="ErrTransactionAborted"></a>ErrTransactionAborted is returned when operations are attempted on an aborted transaction.

```go
var ErrTransactionAborted = &TransactionAbortedError{}
```

<a name="GetValueAs"></a>
## func GetValueAs

```go
func GetValueAs[T any](o *Observable, key string) T
```

GetValueAs returns the value at the given key path, converted to type T. Returns the zero value if the key does not exist. For primitive types, performs a direct type assertion. For structs, uses JSON marshaling to convert from map\[string\]any. The returned value is always a copy. Panics if the conversion fails. Usage: val := observable.GetValueAs\[MyStruct\]\(obs, "config"\)

<a name="ChangeCallback"></a>
## type ChangeCallback

ChangeCallback is the function signature for change notifications. It receives the full key path that changed.

```go
type ChangeCallback func(key string)
```

<a name="Observable"></a>
## type Observable

Observable wraps maps and lists with path\-based access and change subscriptions.

```go
type Observable struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func New

```go
func New() *Observable
```

New creates a new Observable with an empty map as the root.

<a name="NewWithData"></a>
### func NewWithData

```go
func NewWithData(data any) *Observable
```

NewWithData creates a new Observable with the provided data as the root. The data must be nil, a map\[string\]any, or \[\]any.

<a name="Observable.ClearSubscriptions"></a>
### func \(\*Observable\) ClearSubscriptions

```go
func (o *Observable) ClearSubscriptions(subs ...Subscription)
```

ClearSubscriptions removes the specified subscriptions.

<a name="Observable.DeleteValueAtKey"></a>
### func \(\*Observable\) DeleteValueAtKey

```go
func (o *Observable) DeleteValueAtKey(key string) error
```

DeleteValueAtKey removes the value at the given key path. Returns an error if validation fails. This is equivalent to SetValueAtKey\(key, nil\).

<a name="Observable.GetValue"></a>
### func \(\*Observable\) GetValue

```go
func (o *Observable) GetValue(key string) any
```

GetValue returns the value at the given key path, or nil if not found. Key is a dot\-separated path like "x.1.a".

<a name="Observable.OnKeyChange"></a>
### func \(\*Observable\) OnKeyChange

```go
func (o *Observable) OnKeyChange(pattern string, callback ChangeCallback) Subscription
```

OnKeyChange registers a callback to be notified when values at the matching path change. Pattern uses fnmatch\-style matching where \* matches a single segment \(not dots\). Returns the subscription ID for later cleanup.

<a name="Observable.SetValueAtKey"></a>
### func \(\*Observable\) SetValueAtKey

```go
func (o *Observable) SetValueAtKey(key string, value any) error
```

SetValueAtKey sets the value at the given key path, creating intermediate structures as needed. Returns an error if the value violates a schema constraint.

Key is a dot\-separated path like "x.1.a". Numeric path segments indicate array indices, string segments indicate map keys.

Panics if:

- The existing value at a path segment has an incompatible type
- An array index is negative or \>= 100000

<a name="Observable.Transaction"></a>
### func \(\*Observable\) Transaction

```go
func (o *Observable) Transaction(fn func(*Txn)) error
```

Transaction executes a transaction. The callback receives a Txn object to record changes. Changes are automatically committed when the callback returns, unless Abort\(\) is called or an error occurred during the transaction. On commit, all schemas are validated against the final state to catch cross\-field constraint violations that individual updates might not detect. Returns an error if any schema validation fails. Example:

```
err := obs.Transaction(func(tx *Txn) {
    tx.SetValueAtKey("foo", "bar")
    tx.SetValueAtKey("baz", 123)
})
```

<a name="Txn"></a>
## type Txn

Txn represents a transaction that batches changes before applying them. Transactions are created via Observable.Transaction\(\) and are automatically committed when the callback returns, unless Abort\(\) is called.

```go
type Txn struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Txn.Abort"></a>
### func \(\*Txn\) Abort

```go
func (tx *Txn) Abort()
```

Abort cancels the transaction. All recorded changes will be discarded and no notifications will be sent. Subsequent SetValueAtKey calls return ErrTransactionAborted.

<a name="Txn.DeleteValueAtKey"></a>
### func \(\*Txn\) DeleteValueAtKey

```go
func (tx *Txn) DeleteValueAtKey(key string) error
```

DeleteValueAtKey records a deletion to be applied when the transaction commits. Returns an error if validation fails.

<a name="Txn.Err"></a>
### func \(\*Txn\) Err

```go
func (tx *Txn) Err() error
```

Err returns any error that occurred during the transaction.

<a name="Txn.SetValueAtKey"></a>
### func \(\*Txn\) SetValueAtKey

```go
func (tx *Txn) SetValueAtKey(key string, value any) error
```

SetValueAtKey records a change to be applied when the transaction commits. Returns an error if the value violates a schema constraint. If the transaction has an error, the call is ignored and returns ErrTransactionAborted.
